action.reasoning: Observation: The step_2500 checkpoint produced the best success_rate so far (5.0% in iteration 29), outperforming both the 250-step (3.33%) and 5000-step/final (3.33%) checkpoints. Analysis: Different checkpoints have different failure modes; ensembling their predictions before denormalization and bridging should reduce variance and can lift performance. We have three adapters available (step_250, step_2500, final). Decision: Rewrite predict.py to load all available adapters, average their normalized action chunks, then denormalize and apply the same delta→absolute EE bridge. Models are cast to bfloat16 so three 4B-param policies fit in ~24 GB VRAM. Run test_predict.py to verify multi-policy loading and inference, then submit eval for the best checkpoint directory (predict.py will auto-detect and ensemble all available adapters).
